title: Paratephrosia lanata
taxonomy: Paratephrosia lanata is a species of flowering plant in the family Cephalotaceae. It is native to Australia and is a member of the Southern Hemisphere flora. The family Cephalotaceae, to which Paratephrosia lanata belongs, is a group of plants commonly known as water aloe or waterhoods. This family is part of the larger order Saxifragales, which also includes other families such as Crassulaceae and Saxifragaceae. Paratephrosia lanata is an aquatic plant that grows in still or slow-moving water.

intro: Paratephrosia lanata, also known as Lanata Paratephrosia, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Diapensiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Papua New Guinea, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function.
